mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month rise in the CPI above 2.5% signal a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and increases inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds rate and rising CPI are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% and a 2-month decline in payroll employment below 100,000 signal a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate and declining payroll employment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 2-month rise in the RSAFS above 380 million signal a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production and rising RSAFS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.2 million and a 2-month decline in the GDPC above 11650 billion signal a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts and declining GDPC are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
